title: Atropis tenella
intro: Atropis tenella, also known as Tenella, is a plant species in the Apiaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Atropis tenella include coumarins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Atropis tenella is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
